text: And Joseph also went up... unto the city of David, which is called Bethlehem... And she brought forth her firstborn son, and wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger.

event: Paul's conversion on the road to Damascus

text: So Jonah arose, and went unto Nineveh... and he cried, and said, Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown.
Then the fire of the LORD fell, and consumed the burnt sacrifice... And when all the people saw it, they fell on their faces: and they said, The LORD, he is the God.
event: Elijah versus the prophets of Baal

And he said unto him, I am the LORD that brought thee out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.
event: God calls Abram from his homeland

text: So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken unto him... and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.
And after this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah... the same is Hebron in the land of Canaan.
event: Abraham buries Sarah at the cave of Machpelah

text: Then the LORD rained upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the LORD out of heaven.
event: The destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah

text: and she departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.
event: Hagar and Ishmael in the wilderness

text: And he dreamed, and behold a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven: and behold the angels of God ascending and descending on it.
And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.
Now Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well... There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water.
event: Jesus and the woman at the well

text: and the LORD caused the sea to go back by a strong east wind all that night... and the waters were divided.
event: The crossing of the Red Sea

text: And Moses went up... unto the top of Pisgah... And the LORD shewed him all the land.
And the sun stood still, and the moon stayed, until the people had avenged themselves upon their enemies.
Therefore Saul took a sword, and fell upon it.
And he gathered them together into a place called in the Hebrew tongue Armageddon.
And all they in the synagogue... rose up, and thrust him out of the city, and led him unto the brow of the hill... that they might cast him down headlong.
event: Jesus is rejected at his hometown

text: And again he entered into Capernaum... they uncovered the roof where he was... and let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
event: The paralytic lowered through the roof

text: And Jesus, when he was baptized, went up straightway out of the water: and, lo, the heavens were opened unto him.
And was transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth. And he that was dead came forth.
Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.
he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight... Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet.

event: Paul and Silas freed from prison

text: Then Paul stood in the midst of Mars' hill, and said, Ye men of Athens, I perceive that in all things ye are too superstitious.
After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth.
And when we came to Rome... Paul was suffered to dwell by himself with a soldier that kept him.

event: Omri builds the capital of Israel

text: Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard, which was in Jezreel, hard by the palace of Ahab king of Samaria.
The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
event: The faith of the Syrophoenician woman

text: So Hannah rose up... Now Eli the priest sat upon a seat by a post of the temple of the LORD. And she... prayed unto the LORD, and wept sore.
event: Hannah prays for a son at the tabernacle

text: And Samson took hold of the two middle pillars... And Samson said, Let me die with the Philistines. And he bowed himself with all his might.

event: Jeroboam sets up a golden calf

text: And she went and did according to the saying of Elijah... And the barrel of meal wasted not, neither did the cruse of oil fail.

And they returned from searching of the land after forty days... and brought back word... and shewed them the fruit of the land.
event: The twelve spies return from Canaan

text: And he came and touched the bier... And he said, Young man, I say unto thee, Arise. And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak.
And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea... and were choked in the sea.
event: The demons sent into the swine

text: Then he took the five loaves and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed them... And they did all eat, and were filled.
event: The feeding of the five thousand

text: And Peter said unto him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.
And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind... And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness.
And there came thither certain Jews... and, having stoned Paul, drew him out of the city, supposing he had been dead.
event: Paul is stoned and left for dead

text: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica... And Paul, as his manner was, went in unto them.
These were more noble than those in Thessalonica... they received the word with all readiness of mind, and searched the scriptures daily.
event: The noble Bereans search the Scriptures

text: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night; There stood a man of Macedonia, and prayed him, saying, Come over into Macedonia, and help us.
event: Paul's vision of the man of Macedonia

text: And from Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called the elders of the church... Ye know, from the first day that I came into Asia, after what manner I have been with you.
event: Paul's farewell to the Ephesian elders

text: And, hardly passing it, came unto a place which is called The fair havens; nigh whereunto was the city of Lasea.
And unto the angel of the church in Smyrna write... I know thy works, and tribulation, and poverty, (but thou art rich).
event: The letter to the church in Smyrna

text: And to the angel of the church in Pergamos write... I know thy works, and where thou dwellest, even where Satan's seat is.
event: The letter to the church in Pergamum

text: And unto the angel of the church in Sardis write... I know thy works, that thou hast a name that thou livest, and art dead.
event: The letter to the church in Sardis

text: I know thy works, that thou art neither cold nor hot... So then because thou art lukewarm... I will spue thee out of my mouth.
event: The letter to the lukewarm church

text: And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira... whose heart the Lord opened, that she attended unto the things which were spoken of Paul.
event: Lydia, the seller of purple, believes
